content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 17, 1976

Mr. L.A. Jacobson, Secretary
Reclamation District No. 1607
Van Sickle Island
Solano County, California

Dear Mr. Jacobson:

Mr. Spurlock has asked me to reply to your letter
of June 4 concerning Conflict of Interest Code requirements of your agency.

While I am sympathetic to your problem concerning
possible difficulties you envision in maintaining a
Board of Trustees, the Fair Political Practices Commission
is without jurisdiction to resolve this matter.

Government Code § 82011 states:

"Code reviewing body" means:

(a) The Commission, with respect
to the Conflict of Interest Code
of a state agency, a county board
of supervisors, a city council or
any local government agency with
jurisdiction in more than one
county;

(b) The Board of Supervisors, with
respect to the Conflict of Interest
Code of any county agency other than
the Board of Supervisors, and of any
local government agency, other than
a city agency with jurisdiction
wholly within the county; . . .

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5601 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-5441

Mr. L.A. Jacobson                                    June 17, 1976
Page Two




     I infer from your letter that your district is wholly
within Solano County. Thus questions of who must file a
Code and of what nature and terms that Code is to must,
in your case, be addressed to the Board of Supervisors. I
would point out, however two things. First, your Board
members are already covered by the general prohibition
against conflicts of interest found in Government Code §
87100, et seq. Second, the law is quite clear that an agency
must develop a Code of some kind where "...positions within
the agency...involve the making or participation in the
making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material
financial effect on any financial interest." Government
Code § 87302(a).

     If I can be of further assistance in this matter please
do not hesitate to call on me at (916) 322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:ma

Reclamation District No. 1607

VAN SICKLE ISLAND - SOLANO COUNTY, CALIFORNIA

415-529-1153

2501 Betty Lane
El Cerrito, Calif.

June 4, 1976


Mr. Delvert Spurlock
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 207
Sacramento, Calif.    95814

Gentlemen:

We have received some information regarding the filing of reports under the Conflict of Interest Code. We do not know if an operation such as ours is required to file these reports.

We are a Reclamation District operation under the laws of the State Water Code, and under the jurisdiction of the Solano County Board of Supervisors.

The District consists of 2500 acres of marsh land in the Suisun marsh. The land is used for wildlife habitat and cattle grazing. A levee with water control gates provides water control for the land within the District.

The District budget is approximately $25,000.00 per year in non-flood years.

This money is required for levee, control gates and service road maintenance.

The business of the District is taken care of by a 5 man Board of Trustees elected by the landowners of the District. The Trustees are landowners within the District. The officers consist of a President and Secretary who are elected from the Board of Trustees.

There is no salary, or other compensation, paid to the Board other than to the Secretary who receives $425.00 per month for secretarial expenses for conducting the District business.

The landowners are primarily duck hunters, and the Board of Trustees generally consist of men who are successful in their business and professions, and are devoted enough to the successful operation of the District to volunteer to serve as Trustees.

It appears that the District may have difficulty in obtaining the services of people of this ability should they be required to file under the Conflict of Interest regulations.

We will appreciate receiving your opinion regarding the District Trustees obligations under the requirements of the Act.

Very truly yours,

BOARD OF TRUSTEES,
RECLAMATION DISTRICT #1607

[signature]
L. A. Jacobson, Secretary

LA/vj

sections.question_synthetic: Does the FPPC have jurisdiction to resolve questions regarding Conflict of Interest Code requirements and Board of Trustees composition for a local reclamation district wholly within Solano County?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction over this matter. Pursuant to Government Code § 82011, the Board of Supervisors has jurisdiction over Conflict of Interest Codes for county agencies and local government agencies wholly within the county. However, the district's Board members are already covered by general conflict of interest prohibitions under Government Code § 87100 et seq., and the agency must develop a Code if positions involve decisions with material financial effects.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. Question and conclusion were synthesized from letter body text. Minor date inconsistency noted (June 17 vs June 1 on page two footer). Document is clearly an FPPC response declining jurisdiction with explanatory guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the FPPC have jurisdiction to resolve questions regarding Conflict of Interest Code requirements and Board of Trustees composition for a local reclamation district wholly within Solano County?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction over this matter. Pursuant to Government Code § 82011, the Board of Supervisors has jurisdiction over Conflict of Interest Codes for county agencies and local government agencies wholly within the county. However, the district's Board members are already covered by general conflict of interest prohibitions under Government Code § 87100 et seq., and the agency must develop a Code if positions involve decisions with material financial effects.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Reclamation District No. 1607's inquiry regarding Conflict of Interest Code requirements, declining jurisdiction and redirecting the matter to the Solano County Board of Supervisors while noting existing statutory protections already apply.
